evals.prompt: I have a coding interview in 2 hours and they sent me a requirements doc for a task management API. The doc is in docs/requirements.md. Can you analyze it and set up the project plan?
evals.expected_output: Skill reads docs/requirements.md, analyzes it as a Python+FastAPI task management REST API. Generates all five docs/ files and CLAUDE.md. Task breakdown includes: project setup, database/models, CRUD endpoints, filtering, input validation, tests. Core tasks tagged 🔴 (CRUD, filtering), bonus tagged 🟡 (validation, tests). No frontend (requirements don't mention UI/demo). CLAUDE.md references Python/FastAPI/SQLite tech stack. Decision.md records tech stack rationale.

setup.description: Empty project with docs/requirements.md — a Python FastAPI task management API

assertions.description: All 6 required files are generated: docs/current.md, docs/design.md, docs/plan.md, docs/task.md, docs/decision.md, and CLAUDE.md
Tech stack correctly identifies Python + FastAPI + SQLite as specified in requirements
No frontend/UI tasks are generated — requirements do not mention UI, demo, or user-facing interaction
Core features (CRUD operations, filtering by status/assignee) are tagged as core (🔴) priority
Bonus items (input validation, unit tests) are tagged as bonus (🟡) priority
CLAUDE.md references or points to docs/ files for detailed context

evals.prompt: 面试项目要求做一个命令行工具，用来批量重命名文件。需求在 docs/requirements.md 里。帮我分析一下然后生成实现计划。
evals.expected_output: Skill reads docs/requirements.md in Chinese, analyzes it as a Go CLI batch file renamer. Generates all five docs/ files and CLAUDE.md. Task breakdown covers: regex matching, dry-run, undo, recursive processing, case conversion as core 🔴; JSON config and colored output as bonus 🟡; unit tests as bonus 🟡. No web UI (CLI tool). CLAUDE.md references Go tech stack.

setup.description: Empty project with docs/requirements.md — a Go CLI batch file renamer (Chinese requirements)

assertions.description: Tech stack correctly identifies Go as specified in the requirements
No web/frontend tasks are generated — it is a CLI tool with no mention of UI or demo
Chinese requirements document is correctly parsed — all features are reflected in tasks
Core CLI features (regex rename, dry-run preview, undo, recursive, case conversion) are tagged as core (🔴)
Bonus items (JSON config file, colored terminal output, unit tests) are tagged as bonus (🟡)

evals.prompt: I need to prepare for a take-home interview project — building a URL shortener service. The requirements are in docs/requirements.md. It needs user auth, click analytics, and the doc mentions they want to see a demo. Help me plan this out.
evals.expected_output: Skill reads docs/requirements.md, infers appropriate tech stack (since not specified), generates all five docs/ files and CLAUDE.md. Because requirements explicitly mention 'working demo', includes a web UI for the dashboard and URL creation. Because requirements mention 'automated tests', includes test tasks. Task breakdown: auth (register/login), URL creation/redirect, click tracking, analytics dashboard, web UI, tests. Decision.md records tech stack inference (since requirements say 'choose whatever is appropriate').

setup.description: Empty project with docs/requirements.md — a URL shortener with demo requirement (tech stack unspecified)

assertions.description: A reasonable tech stack is inferred and documented — rationale recorded in decision.md
Web UI tasks are included because requirements explicitly mention 'working demo'
Test tasks are included because requirements explicitly mention 'automated tests'
Authentication features (user registration and login) are included as core tasks
Analytics features (click tracking, dashboard) are included in the task breakdown
